title: Apartment Finder
description: Built a personal AI search app that turns noisy Buenos Aires rental listings into evidence-backed decisions about likely in-unit washing machines.
bestFit: Founders and small teams turning messy real-world search, review, or triage workflows into focused AI-assisted products.
constraints: Listing sites often mark shared building laundry as a washer amenity, even when the decision depends on whether the washer is inside the private apartment.
The app needed to work across Zonaprop, Argenprop, and Airbnb while keeping enough evidence visible to audit the model's judgment.
problem: Apartment search filters were producing too many false matches because rental platforms collapse private in-unit washers and shared building laundry into the same amenity signal.
The real decision required inspecting listing photos, provider metadata, price and room details, descriptions, and ambiguous evidence across many listings without opening every gallery by hand.
solution: Built a search app that accepts provider filters or raw result URLs, discovers listings, extracts gallery photos and listing details, and scans each listing for washer evidence.

Wrapped the classifier in a product UI with provider filters, neighborhood typeahead, async scan progress, decision filters, price sorting, expandable result rows, extracted descriptions, amenity details, evidence links, and a listing-photo lightbox.
Kept the underlying CLI and JSONL workflow for repeatable evals, audit trails, and provider extraction smoke tests.
result: Validated the core listing-summary workflow at 53/55 on labeled listings, with zero false in-unit and zero false shared-building decisions in the latest run.
Reduced second-pass model usage by switching to targeted top-k escalation: the selected 6-photo cap cut escalated image checks from roughly 969 to 247 while preserving the best measured 53/55 result.

Turned a frustrating personal search problem into a reusable product pattern: extract messy source data, classify the hard evidence, show the user why the system made the call, and keep the workflow auditable.
Shipped the project publicly as an open-source example of building an AI product around evidence and workflow design, not just a model call.
